mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.14%) signals persistently accommodative monetary policy that keeps equity discount rates low, supporting continued S&P 500 appreciation over the following month absent a policy shift.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS is below 1% versus above 1% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) exceeding a threshold signals weakening labor demand, prompting risk-off repricing of equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below +100,000 after prior 3-month average above +200,000
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered instances are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline or stagnation in industrial production (INDPRO) during an industrial slowdown regime reflects deteriorating manufacturing-sector fundamentals and forecasts negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO flat-to-declining through 2015 amid strong dollar and energy-sector capex cuts
regime_hint: industrial/manufacturing slowdown regime (e.g., 2015 energy-and-dollar-driven soft patch)
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive on average during periods of INDPRO 3-month annualized decline greater than 1%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates simultaneous consumer spending momentum and housing-sector confidence, a combination associated with broad-based domestic demand expansion that supports S&P 500 gains over the next month more strongly than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 2% AND HOUST above 1,100k units simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows forward 20-day returns statistically indistinguishable from the single-series triggers (RSAFS-only or HOUST-only), indicating no incremental interaction effect (interaction coefficient p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate begins rising from near-zero levels after a prolonged easing cycle, markets price in tighter future liquidity conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as discount rates rise faster than earnings expectations adjust.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises more than 0.10pp within a single month after 12+ months near 0.0-0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:market anticipation of first rate hike (liftoff) in late 2015
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following a liftoff trigger are positive in more than 55% of historical liftoff analogs, or average triggered return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady positive monthly nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market expansion that sustains consumer income and spending, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 prices over the following week.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward SPX return conditional on positive PAYEMS growth is not significantly greater than the unconditional average (p > 0.05) across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its trailing trend signals diminishing economic momentum, leading investors to discount future corporate earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 growth moderating through mid-2015 amid global slowdown concerns
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during GDPC1 deceleration quarters (QoQ annualized growth below trailing 4-quarter average) are not significantly lower than in acceleration quarters (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero federal funds rate and a falling unemployment rate approaching levels consistent with full employment creates policy-tightening risk: markets anticipate the Fed will hike rates sooner than priced, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month when both conditions co-occur, an effect not present when either signal is absent.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% AND UNRATE below 5.5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% in Sept 2015 nearing Fed's full-employment estimates while FEDFUNDS remained near zero
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly different from periods where only one condition holds, or interaction term coefficient in a regression of returns on both series is statistically insignificant (p > 0.1).
